rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LENA MAE RENFRO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/31/2024, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (see tentative ruling for 12/06/2023 hearing): 1) An original small estate affidavit signed by each successor of Esma Renfro (who survived the decedent and whose interest in this estate vested) must be filed with a certified copy of the death certificate. This document CANNOT be e-filed and must be filed conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached. 2) An original small estate affidavit signed by each successor of James Renfro (who survived the decedent and whose interest in this estate vested) must be filed with a certified copy of the death certificate. This document CANNOT be e-filed and must be filed conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached. 3) An original small estate affidavit signed by each successor of Belinda Renfro (who survived the decedent and James Renfro and whose interest in this estate vested) must be filed with a certified copy of the death certificate. This document CANNOT be e-filed and must be filed conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached. Note: Belinda Renfro's successors must sign two affidavits: one for the estate of James Renfro; and one for the estate of Belinda Renfro. See Probate Code sections 11801(a), 11802(c), and 13100 et seq; see also LRSF 14.35(G) & 14.59(C)(2)(6). If a small estate affidavit cannot be obtained from all the successors (or cannot be used for any other reason), distribution must be made under section 11802(a). A certified copy of letters issued to the personal representative within 60 days before the hearing must be conventionally filed with a pleading cover sheet attached (CANNOT be e-filed), or a certification of authority for summary administration issued by a public administrator under section 7660(a)(2)(A) must be filed if distribution is made to the public administrator acting as personal representative to proceed with summary disposition.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF CONNIE M. MARATAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/4/24. (No earlier availability.) (1) Notice was mailed to the conservatee on 10/4/23 at an old address. Conservator was authorized on 3/13/23 to move Conservatee to Family Affair Home Care. (2) The verification of this (second) amended petition is incomplete (not dated). (3) The conservator was appointed on 7/17/19, not 7/7/19. (4) The total bond is $1,187,000, not $1,287,000. (5) It is unclear how the value of the Genworth Long Term Care at the end of the accounting period was determined. The amount shown as the CV and FMV was not on the statement. (6) It is also unclear if the conservator is requesting that she be authorized not to pay the Sage Eldercare bill or to pay some portion of it the amount was not in the body of the petition, and the prayer is vague. It is not the Court's duty to discern if care costs are reasonable for which a conservator has contracted. (7) One of the pages of the conservator's time entries is cut off. Deadline for providing the assigned examiner with courtesy copies of any responsive pleadings is 2/22/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CONNIE MARY MARATAS TRUST 1997; AND THE CONNIE M. MARATAS REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST 2014
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/4/24. (No earlier availability.) (1) Notice was mailed to the current beneficiary at an address that differs from the one set forth in the pleading. (2) The last known address of Tamara Seebach-Morgan, a contingent remainder beneficiary, was not set forth in the pleading. [CRC 7.902.] She is also still missing from the notice service list. (3) The verification of the pleading is incomplete (not dated) and was signed by "Conserv." (4) The schedule of assets on hand at the beginning of the account period (p. 3 of 4) shows the incorrect number of shares of Royce Total Return Fund and Tweedy Browne Value Fund. (5) The total CV of Royce Total Return on hand as of 3/31/21 appears to be slightly off if the value of reinvested dividends was $200.47, and the 6,035.713 shares on hand at the beginning of the account period were appraised at $56,132.13. (6) Court-conformed accountings are "cash" accountings. It is unclear how reinvested dividends were accounted for as both receipts and shown on the informational schedule increasing the carry value. (7) This is the third amended petition, but the trustee still claims that the house is being prepared to be rented and the same typographical errors and incorrect dates continue to be made or stated as in the prior three versions: the former attorney was Jennifer MARLOWE (and HER declaration was filed on 8/17/23, not with this third amended petition); the filing history of prior versions of this fully amended petition or of ex parte petitions to increase bond and reporting that copies were provided to the Court are not actions that need to be reported in an accounting petition; and the successor trustee was appointed on 12/10/20 (the order was not filed until 12/15/20). It WOULD be helpful, however, if the date when a "previously filed" fee declaration were set forth (see paragraph 34). (8) Paragraph 35.A. shows that the current attorney worked 18.9 hours on this case An amendment to this third amended petition should be filed to address just these issues. Notice of the amendment to the third amended petition must be served at least 30 days before 3/4/24 after the amendment is e-filed. If another fully amended petition is filed, it must include an accounting for the period ending 12/9/22, as this accounting period is now severely outdated. Deadline is 2/22/24 for providing the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any responsive pleadings. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (After these issues are addressed, an appearance will likely be required for the Court to determine if notice to the conservatee/beneficiary should be dispensed with and if legal fees are reasonable. Giving notice to a conserved person is still required by law and notice merely indicates that a hearing will take place and does not disclose the assets in an estate. There was no hearing on 4/11/22, and several time entries were a result of pleadings being incomplete or incorrect, as with this one. "Good faith" is not grounds for awarding legal fees; legal fees must be just, reasonable, and in the best interest of the conservatee. [Probate Code Section 2623(a)(1). See also CRC 7.751 and 7.702.])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TONI MARIE MOORE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, notwithstanding the paralegal's declaration re the correct statutory fee base calculation, where a Court-appointed fiduciary - who verifies her filed pleadings - is required to sign and verify all of her own pleadings, including any supplement or amendment to her pleadings, regardless of who made the clerical error therein. [Probate Code Section 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).] NB: Court does not ordinarily allow fees for legal research. No SASE was provided.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YVONNE WILLIAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Amdd PLA filed 12/27/23 Continued to 3/4/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) An amended petition was apparently just filed on 12/27/23, which was not at least 15 days before this hearing for proper notice to be given. (2) The proof of service filed on 12/27/23 does not show that an AMENDED petition has been filed. (3) The proof of service claims that notice was mailed on 9/1/23, but if that was for the original petition, it is defective because an amended petition has been filed that supersedes the original petition. (4) No proof of publication has been filed yet. (5) No Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) has been filed yet. Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (6) The caption of the amended petition and item 2a of the amended petition indicate that there is a will, but no will has been lodged and a copy of the will is not attached to the petition as Attachment 3f(2). (7) The amended petition states that the decedent died on 5/24/21. The proposed order shows the date of death as 6/4/20. (8) Item 3g(2) of the petition should not be completed if there is a will. ("Beneficiary" is not a relationship to the decedent.) (9) Item 5a of the amended petition is still incomplete. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 2/22/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE MATILDA CYTRON TRUST U/D/T DATED MARCH 20, 1978, as amended
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition 1 For Order Determining Questions Of Interpretation Of Trust Probate Code 17200b1 And Instructing Trustee To Follow Terms Of Trust 2) For Order Compelling Trustee To Account In Accordance With Probate Code Sections 1060-1064; 3) For Redress Of Breach Of Trust 4) For Order Removing Trustee; 5) Order Allowing Attorney Feess And Coscts
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/28/2024 (no earlier date available), as the following issues (see tentative rulings for 10/25/2023 & 12/06/2023 hearings) remain unaddressed (or must be additionally addressed upon review of supplemental papers): 1) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 30 days before the hearing. The proof of service attached to the DE-120 form and filed on 12/28/2023 shows notice was given on 12/27/2023, less than 30 days before this hearing. New 30-day notice of the 02/28/2024 hearing must be given. 2) The address of each beneficiary listed on the proof of service must be confirmed by a verified allegation. Probate Code section 17201 & CRC 7.902. 3) The date of death of Daniel Cytron must be stated by a verified allegation. If he survived Shirley Cytron, the personal representative of his estate must be identified by a verified allegation and receive notice. If no personal representative has been appointed, a verified allegation must be made as to whether he died testate or intestate, and his heirs (if he died intestate) including his surviving spouse if any, or devisees and executors named in his will (if he died testate), must be identified by a verified allegation and receive notice. See section 17203(c). If Daniel failed to survive Shirly, Daniel's surviving issue must be identified by a verified allegation and receive notice. 4) If Lenora's ownership interest in the Benedict Canyon Property at the time of her death in 2007 was 48.74%, please explain, by a verified allegation, why Shirley's ownership interest in the Benedict Canyon Property at the time of her death in 2019 was 42.685%, less than 48.74% Shirley presumably held at the time of Lenora's death. Note: Supplemental papers containing verified allegations must be signed by the petitioner with language required by CCP section 2015.5. If verified by counsel, langauge required by section 1023 and CRC 7.103(c) must be included.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE CHUNG HOP WONG AND KING TSUI LAU WONG REVOCABL TRUST dated sep 21, 2009 amended Sep 26 2009
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Compensatory Damages Against Kathryn Wong For Elder Abuse; Undue Influence; Conversion; Misrepresentation; Misappropriation; Breach Of Fi

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JOURDIN LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Instruct Trustee To Sell Real Property Or Alternatively To Remove And Replace Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. *REPOSTED 12/28/23 at 3:00 pm* Continued to 3/13/2024 at the counsels' request. The following issues to be cleared prior to the next hearing: Procedural Issues on Petition: (1) Need allegation regarding the nature of the settlor's email and NEED Clarification on the petitioner's position. For example, if it should be considered as a testamentary document. If so, what the document should be (amendment to the trust, a newer will, a separate memo etc.) (2) Need clarification how sec.850 is applicable here when the real property is possessed by the trust which seems to be the rightful owner. (3) Also, service of Notice of Hearing failed to meet sec. 851(a) as the notice [DE-115] with a copy of petition was only mailed to the trustee. (4) Petitioner's position to be updated as Respondent seems to have performed what Petition prays for. Procedural Issue on Response/Answer: Need Proof of service PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PERRY L. AND GLORIA J. WASH FAMILY TRUST DATED FEB TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Judicial Determination Of Subtrust Allocation, For Order Confirming Effect Of Exercise Of Power Of Appointment, For Order Confirming Distributive Shares To Beneficiaries, And For Order Determining Vadility Of Trust Amendement
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. The petition has not been reviewed. The sole issue for hearing will be whether venue is proper in San Francisco. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LARRY DEAN COATE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/31/2024, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 10/27/2023 - see also tentative ruling for 11/27/2023 hearing): 1) What day of February 2017 did the petitioner see the decedent sign and acknowledge the will? 2) Jennifer Walters' complete zip code must be listed at item 8. 3) Arithinat Surawatthana's address listed at item 8 must match the address listed (in English) on the proof of service attached to the DE-121 form. 4) Attachment 2 must describe anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate, even if the total amount of such liabilities and claims does not exceed $20,000. 5) If the petitioner is one of the only two witnesses to the will, allegations must be made to rebut the presumption under section 6112(c) that the petitioner procured her devise by duress, menace, fraud, or undue influence, as the issue may affect the petitioner's eligibility for appointment under sections 8402(a)(3) & 8502.
